name: wiki-page-writer
description: Generates rich technical documentation pages with dark-mode Mermaid diagrams, source code citations, and first-principles depth. Use when writing documentation, generating wiki pages, creating technical deep-dives, or documenting specific components or systems.
capabilities: Generates comprehensive technical documentation pages with structured sections
Creates dark-mode compatible Mermaid diagrams including sequence, graph, class, and flowchart types
Cites specific file paths and line numbers for all technical claims
Traces actual code paths by analyzing implementation files
Produces VitePress-compatible markdown with proper frontmatter
Includes comparison tables, API documentation, and pseudocode examples
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer detected 34 patterns (external_commands, weak_crypto, reconnaissance) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains only markdown documentation syntax with code blocks and YAML frontmatter examples. No executable code, network calls, or cryptographic operations exist. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Creating comprehensive technical documentation requires significant time and consistent formatting. This skill automates wiki page generation with evidence-based citations, dark-mode Mermaid diagrams, and VitePress-compatible structure for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
userTitle: Generate Technical Documentation with Mermaid Diagrams
limitations: Cannot execute code or verify documentation against running systems
Requires access to source code files for accurate analysis and citations
May require human review for domain-specific accuracy in complex systems
Generates markdown and Mermaid output only, does not deploy or host documentation

useCases.title: Document CI/CD Pipeline Architecture
useCases.description: Generate comprehensive documentation for a CI/CD pipeline with sequence diagrams showing build stages, component citations, and deployment flow visualizations.

useCases.targetUser: Open source maintainers building project documentation
useCases.title: Create Open Source Feature Documentation
useCases.description: Produce detailed feature documentation for open source projects with architecture diagrams, API reference tables, and code path citations for community contributors.

useCases.targetUser: Engineering teams creating internal knowledge bases
useCases.title: Build Internal Technical Wiki
useCases.description: Generate consistent internal documentation pages for engineering teams with standardized diagrams, evidence-based citations, and searchable structure.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Component Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a documentation page for the [component name] in [file path]. Include an overview, architecture diagram, and cite at least 3 source files with line numbers.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick documentation for a single component or module

promptTemplates.title: Full Feature Deep-Dive
promptTemplates.prompt: Write comprehensive documentation for the [feature name] feature. Analyze all files in [directory path], create 3+ Mermaid diagrams (sequence, class, and flowchart), trace the complete data flow, and cite specific code paths with file:line references.
promptTemplates.scenario: Detailed documentation for complex features requiring architectural understanding

promptTemplates.title: API Reference with Examples
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate API reference documentation for [module/service]. Include endpoint tables with parameters and return types, usage examples in code blocks, and sequence diagrams showing request flows. Cite implementation files for each endpoint.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating API documentation for internal or external consumption

promptTemplates.title: System Migration Guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a migration guide from [old system] to [new system]. Include architecture comparison diagrams, component mapping tables, data flow changes, and step-by-step migration procedures with code citations from both systems.
promptTemplates.scenario: Documenting system migrations or refactoring efforts

bestPractices: Always trace actual code paths before documenting functionality
Use consistent dark-mode colors in Mermaid diagrams for visual coherence
Cite specific file paths and line numbers for every technical claim
antiPatterns: Making claims about functionality without reading the actual implementation code
Using Mermaid syntax that breaks dark-mode rendering or VitePress compatibility
Generating placeholder documentation without evidence-based source citations

faq.question: What types of files can this skill document?
faq.answer: This skill can document any codebase accessible to your AI assistant. It analyzes source files to generate documentation with accurate citations and diagrams.

faq.question: Does this skill create diagrams automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It generates dark-mode compatible Mermaid diagrams including sequence diagrams, class diagrams, flowcharts, and state diagrams based on the code structure.

faq.question: How are citations formatted in the documentation?
faq.answer: Citations use the format (file_path:line_number) after technical claims, allowing readers to verify information by checking the source code directly.

faq.question: Is the output compatible with VitePress?
faq.answer: Yes. Output includes proper VitePress frontmatter, escaped generics in code blocks, and Mermaid syntax that renders correctly in VitePress sites.

faq.question: Can this skill execute code to verify documentation?
faq.answer: No. This skill analyzes source code statically and generates documentation. It does not execute code or verify behavior against running systems.

faq.question: What if the skill cannot access certain files?
faq.answer: When files are inaccessible, the skill marks inferences clearly and uses citations like (Unknown - verify in path/to/check) to indicate unverified claims.
